text: Object tracking from videos has drawn increasing attention in recent years and most of the works focus on how to model the spatial-temporal relationships between objects. In this work, we propose a novel model, named as TransMOT, which adopts graph transformers to model object relationships. In particular, the tracking trajectories of multiple objects are constructed as a set of sparse weighted graphs, and then adopted to build up a spatial graph transformer encoder layer, a temporal transformer encoder layer and a spatial graph transformer decoder layer to achieve efficient and effective object relationships modeling. Compared with the traditional Transformer, our proposed TransMOT has higher computational efficiency while maintaining competitive performance. To further improve the real-time performance and tracking accuracy, the cascade association framework is introduced to deal with low-score detections and handle the long-term occlusions that needs to consume a lot of computational resources in TransMOT. In the experiments, the proposed model achieves the state-of-the-art performances on MOT15, MOT16, MOT17 and MOT20 benchmarks datasets.
